data.body:
## Completion Verification: PASS

Verified all four acceptance criteria against the implementation. Every criterion is met.

---

### AC1 — Eval solver injects per-case ticket/graph context + stubs the agentic file tools (no live store/repo dependency)
**MET.**

`src/rebar/llm/eval_solver.py` (lines 1–105) implements `run_case(prompt_id, case, *, runner)` and `case_store(case)`.

- `case_store()` (lines 55–63): opens a `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory`, calls `_git_init()` + `rebar.init_repo(repo_root=d)` to create a disposable store, and writes fixture files via `_write_files()` — no live store touched.
- `run_case()` (lines 66–105): for each of the three reviewers it creates a synthetic ticket in the disposable store (`rebar.create_ticket(... repo_root=root)`) from `case["ticket_context"]` / `case["epics"]`, then calls the real op with `repo_root=root` and `runner=runner` — so the op's own `show_ticket`/file tools read only the disposable, seeded store. The `runner` seam (a `FakeRunner` offline, live runner in CI) intercepts all model calls.
- All three branches are present: `completion-verifier` → `completion.verify_completion(tid, repo_root=root, runner=runner)` (line 80); `ticket-quality` → `operations.review_ticket(tid, repo_root=root, runner=runner)` (line 87); `spec-alignment` → `spec_scan.scan_epics_for_spec(..., repo_root=root, runner=runner)` (line 102–103).

---

### AC2 — Versioned fixture corpus backs cases that need repo state (esp. completion-verifier citations)
**MET.**

`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/completion-verifier.eval.yaml` ships 6 dataset cases (lines 45–147), each with a `files:` section (at lines 53, 76, 89, 101, 126, 139). Representative examples:
- `CV-missing-ac-item`: `files: {src/cli.py: ...}` — implements `foo` subcommand without `--json` flag.
- `CV-bug-unfixed`: `files: {app/checkout.py: ...}` — discount_code never applied.
- `CV-missing-test`: `files: {src/util.py: ...}` — `add()` helper exists but no test file.
- Three `pass` cases with complete implementations.

`eval_solver._write_files()` (lines 36–44) writes these into the git-backed disposable repo and commits them, making them visible to the real file tools the verifier exercises.

---

### AC3 — Ground-truth labels adjudicated and recorded; seed labels marked provisional
**MET.**

`completion-verifier.eval.yaml` line 42 (comment block above `dataset:`): `"(Seed fixtures; labels provisional pending human adjudication, per README.plan-review.md.)"` — provisional convention explicitly stated.

`gold_set` (lines 148–157) records 4 labeled entries (`useful`/`not_useful`) following the established pattern in `README.plan-review.md` (lines 36–39: "best-effort SEED pending human adjudication"). The ticket-quality (`ticket-quality.eval.yaml` lines 90–98) and spec-alignment (`spec-alignment.eval.yaml` lines 87–95) specs likewise carry non-empty `gold_set` sections. The plan-review-finder spec (lines 179–180) carries the explicit "SEED labels — pending human adjudication" marker; the completion-verifier spec follows the same convention per its comment.

---

### AC4 — Tests exercise the solver against FakeRunner/FakeAgentRunner (offline, no tokens)
**MET.**

`tests/unit/test_eval_solver.py` has 6 tests, all using `FakeRunner` (defined at `runner.py:98`), offline with no model calls:

| Test | Reviewer | FakeRunner payload |
|---|---|---|
| `test_completion_verifier_case_runs_and_scores` | completion-verifier | `structured={verdict, findings}` |
| `test_ticket_quality_case_runs_and_scores` | ticket-quality | `findings=[{severity, dimension, detail}]` |
| `test_ticket_quality_good_case_no_fire` | ticket-quality | `findings=[]` |
| `test_spec_alignment_batch_case_runs` | spec-alignment | `findings=[{severity, dimension, detail}]` |
| `test_fixture_files_are_written_into_the_store` | n/a (case_store) | n/a |
| `test_unknown_prompt_raises` | n/a (error path) | `FakeRunner()` |

Each test also validates that the scorer registry (`eval_scorers.score(...)`) can score the solver's output, confirming end-to-end wiring.

---

**Overall: PASS** — all four acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied by the implementation.
